name: add_template
description: pastes the specified template at the cursor location

commands.title: set-template [ template-name ]
commands.body: When this command is run the template that is passed will be pasted at the current cursor location

notes: The user must add templates to the ~/.yed/templates dir. The files must be named in this style [template-name.txt].

name: align
description: Align all first occurences of a string in a set of lines.

commands.title: align <string>
commands.body: Align all of the first occurences of <string> in the selected lines to the rightmost match in the set.

name: auto_paren
description: automatically adds the matching character to these [({'"

configuration.title: disable-auto-paren   "  " [ yes / no ]
configuration.body: disables the automatic insert of a following )

configuration.title: disable-auto-bracket ""   [ yes / no ]
configuration.body: disables the automatic insert of a following ]

configuration.title: disable-auto-dquote  " "  [ yes / no ]
configuration.body: disables the automatic insert of a following "

configuration.title: disable-auto-quote   "  " [ yes
configuration.body: disables the automatic insert of a following '

configuration.title: disable-auto-brace   "  " [ yes / no ]
configuration.body: disables the automatic insert of a following }

configuration.title: auto-paren-skip      "  " [ yes / no ]
configuration.body: if set you will be able to skip ]}) no matter where you are in the file

configuration.title: auto-dquote-skip     "  " [ yes / no ]
configuration.body: if set you will be able to skip " no matter where you are in the file

configuration.title: auto-quote-skip      "  " [ yes / no ]
configuration.body: if set you will be able to skip ' no matter where you are in the file

configuration.title: auto-paren-jump_word "  " [ yes / no ]
configuration.body: if set you will be able to move the )]} to the right side of the connected word

configuration.title: auto-dquote-jump_word "  " [ yes / no ]
configuration.body: if set you will be able to move the " to the right side of the connected word

configuration.title: auto-quote-jump_word "  " [ yes / no ]
configuration.body: if set you will be able to move the ' to the right side of the connected word

configuration.title: auto-quote-selection "  " [ yes / no ]
configuration.body: if set you will be able to insert '' around the selection

configuration.title: auto-dquote-selection "  " [ yes / no ]
configuration.body: if set you will be able to insert "" around the selection

configuration.title: auto-paren-selection "  " [ yes / no ]
configuration.body: if set you will be able to insert () around the selection

configuration.title: auto-brace-selection "  " [ yes / no ]
configuration.body: if set you will be able to insert [] around the selection

notes:
Automatically adds the correct following character to the one inserted.
Auto split and indention has been added to ([{ when you hit enter.

name: auto-save
description: Periodically check for modified buffers and automatically save them.

configuration.title: auto-save-frequency-ms <ms>
configuration.body: The number of milliseconds before all buffers are checked for modifications and saved. The minimum allowed is 10,000 milliseconds (10 seconds).

commands.title: auto-save-force-quit
commands.body: Force quit without checking any buffers and without saving.

notes: This overrides the built-in quit command to automatically save modified buffers and then quit. It is recommended to NOT use this plugin alongside confirm-quit.

name: autotrim
description: Automatically removes trailing whitespace from lines whenever you save a buffer, keeping your files clean.
notes: This plugin runs automatically on every buffer write. No configuration needed — just load it and trailing whitespace is handled.

name: bookmarks
description: bookmark lines of code to return to later

configuration.title: bookmark-character "                       " [ ▓ ]
configuration.body: the character displayed in the gutter to indicate a bookmark

configuration.title: bookmark-use-line-numbers "                " [ (0) / 1 ]
configuration.body: enable or disable line numbers in the gutter

configuration.title: bookmark-color "                           " [ &blue ]
configuration.body: the color used for the bookmark indicator in the gutter

configuration.title: bookmark-path-color "                      " [ &green ]
configuration.body: the color used for file paths in the bookmarks list buffer

configuration.title: bookmark-row-color "                       " [ &purple ]
configuration.body: the color used for row numbers in the bookmarks list buffer

configuration.title: bookmark-line-color "                      " [ &gray ]
configuration.body: the color used for line contents in the bookmarks list buffer

configuration.title: bookmark-update-period "                   " [ 5 ]
configuration.body: how often (in seconds) the bookmarks list buffer refreshes

configuration.title: bookmarks-file "                           " [ unset ]
configuration.body: path (relative to cwd) to the file used for persisting bookmarks

configuration.title: bookmark-highlight-line "                  " [ (0) / 1 ]
configuration.body: enable or disable highlighting the entire bookmarked line

configuration.title: bookmark-highlight-line-color "            " [ &blue swap ]
configuration.body: the color used for highlighting bookmarked lines

configuration.title: line-number-scomp "                        " [ code-comment ]
configuration.body: style component used for line numbers

commands.title: set-bookmark(row)
commands.body: set a new bookmark at the given row

commands.title: remove-bookmark(row)
commands.body: remove the bookmark at the given row

commands.title: set-bookmark-on-line
commands.body: set a new bookmark at the current cursor line

commands.title: remove-bookmark-on-line
commands.body: remove the bookmark at the current cursor line

commands.title: toggle-bookmark-on-line
commands.body: toggle a bookmark at the current cursor line

commands.title: remove-all-bookmarks-in-buffer
commands.body: remove all bookmarks in the current buffer

commands.title: remove-all-bookmarks
commands.body: remove all bookmarks across all buffers

commands.title: goto-next-bookmark
commands.body: move the cursor to the next bookmark across all buffers

commands.title: goto-prev-bookmark
commands.body: move the cursor to the previous bookmark across all buffers

commands.title: goto-next-bookmark-in-buffer
commands.body: move the cursor to the next bookmark in the current buffer

commands.title: goto-prev-bookmark-in-buffer
commands.body: move the cursor to the previous bookmark in the current buffer

commands.title: bookmarks
commands.body: open the *bookmarks-list special buffer

buffers.title: *bookmarks-list -- a read-only buffer listing all bookmarks, press Enter to jump to a bookmark

notes: ### Bookmarks pointing past the end of a file are automatically removed when the file is loaded.

name: brace_hl
description: Visually highlights the matching curly brace when your cursor is on one, useful for tracking scope in C-style languages.

configuration.title: brace-hl-max-line-length
configuration.body: When scanning for matching braces, if a line exceeds brace-hl-max-line-length, stop scanning.

notes: Uses the "attention" style component.

name: builder
description: Run build commands in the background. Jump to error if it fails.

configuration.title: builder-build-command <command>
configuration.body: The command to run when you run builder-start.

configuration.title: builder-error-parse-command <command>
configuration.body:
The command responsible for parsing error locations from the command output.
The default value can parse error locations from most C/C++ compilers.

configuration.title: builder-popup-rg [yes/no]
configuration.body:
Sets the builder popup to be either green or red on success or failure.

See NOTES.

configuration.title: builder-notify-command <command>
configuration.body:
A command that can be run when the build command finishes.
A small popup will display momentarily if this is unset.

See NOTES.

commands.title: builder-start
commands.body: Start the builder-build-command in the background.

commands.title: builder-jump-to-error
commands.body: Jump to the error location parsed from the command output.

commands.title: builder-print-error
commands.body: Print the error text on the command line if the build failed.

commands.title: builder-view-output
commands.body: Focus the *builder-output buffer.

commands.title: builder-view-echo-status
commands.body: Print the status of the build on the command line.

notes:
### Parsing Errors

The builder-error-parse-command should be a shell command that, when invoked with the builder-build-command's output on STDIN, produces output lines of the format FILE:LINE:COLUMN:ERROR for lines that report an error.
The default value of builder-error-parse-command is given below as an example:

```
    awk -F':' '
        /:( fatal)? error:/     { print $0; }
        /: undefined reference/ { printf("%s:%s:1:%s\n", $1, $2, $3); }'
```
### Notification
The builder-notify-command should be a shell command with a non-backslash-escaped % symbol which will be replaced by the status of the build.
For example:

```
    ./email_builder_status '%'
```

name: calc
description: An interactive calculator in a special buffer with expression history, custom variables, and common math operations.

commands.title: calc
commands.body: Focus the *calc buffer.

notes:
Each expression typed into the calculator is added to the history list and can be reused by its ans_* name.
Variables can be set by typing an expression of the form "name = expression".

name: command-history
description: Saves commands between one use of YED and another.

configuration.title: command-history-limit <limit>
configuration.body: The number of commands to be saved for command history. The maximum allowed is 10,000.

notes: The file that this plugin uses to store the command history is in your config directory..

name: comment
description: Toggle line comments on and off with a single command. Automatically detects the correct comment syntax for your filetype.

configuration.title: <FT>-style-comments "PREFIX [SUFFIX]"
configuration.body:
This plugin will look for variables of this pattern to decide how to comment different filetypes.
For example, for C-comment-style "/* */", lines will be surrounded by the "/* " prefix and " */" suffix.
Alternatively, C-comment-style "//", will simply prefix lines with "// ".

commands.title: comment-toggle
commands.body: Toggles the current line. If there is a selection, toggles every line in the selection.

notes: FT plugins should set the corresponding variables. However, users can also override these.

name: completer
description: An inline completion popup that shows suggestions as you type, drawing from buffer words, ctags, and LSP sources.

configuration.title: completer-sources [ sources... ]
configuration.body: Space-delimited list of completion sources to use. Default is "word".

configuration.title: completer-popup [ yes / no ]
configuration.body:
Whether or not do display the popup menu for completion results.
Default value is "yes".

configuration.title: completer-popup-size [ int ]
configuration.body:
The size in lines of the popup menu.
 Default value is 8.

configuration.title: completer-auto [ yes / no ]
configuration.body:
Whether or not the popup should come up automatically as you type in an "autocomplete" fashion.
Default value is "no".

notes: Completer uses the word ([a-Z0-9_]+) before the cursor as the search string.

name: confirm-quit
description: Check for unsaved buffers before quitting.

commands.title: no-confirm-quit
commands.body: Force quit without checking for modified buffers. Changed in unsaved buffers will be lost.

notes: This overrides the built-in quit command.

name: convert
description: Convert numbers between decimal, hex, octal, and binary formats, or convert strings between different encodings.

commands.title: convert-number [ template-name ]
commands.body:
When this command is run the current word the currsor is on will be parsed.
If it is a number, a popup will show and allow the user to replace the current word with a conversion.
Converts numbers between Decimal, Hex, Octal, Binary.
If the current word is a string.
A popup will show and allow the user to swap between Uppercase, Lowercase, Snakecase, and Camelcase.

notes: The formats are as follows, Decimal is any number less than '18446744073709551615' and greater than '-9223372036854775808', Hex must start with '0x' or '0X', Binary must start with '0b', and Octal must start with '0'

name: ctags
description: ctags integration for yed. Provides symbol generation, highlighting, completion, searching, and jumping.

configuration.title: ctags-tags-file [ path ]
configuration.body: The name of the tags file to use/generate. Default is "tags".

configuration.title: ctags-flags [ flags ]
configuration.body: Specifies the flags to pass to ctags when running ctags-gen.

configuration.title: ctags-additional-paths [ path... ]
configuration.body: A list of extra files/directories to produce tags for. This is useful if, for example, you want to include library header files.

configuration.title: ctags-fallback-autogen [ yes / no ]
configuration.body: If this variable is true, temporary tags will be automatically generated if a tags file does not exist and you have not run ctags-gen yourself. The files in the current working directory, any files in ./src, and any open buffers will be used as the sources of the tags.

configuration.title: ctags-regen-on-write [ yes / no ]
configuration.body: If this variable is true, tags will be automatically regenerated when you write a buffer.

configuration.title: ctags-auto-show-function-hint [ yes / no ]
configuration.body: If this variable is true, function argument hints will pop up automatically.

configuration.title: ctags-auto-dismiss-function-hint [ yes / no ]
configuration.body: If this variable is true, function argument hints go away when your cursor leaves the function call.

configuration.title: ctags-formatting-limit [ int ]
configuration.body: The maximum number of tags entries in *ctags-find before it stops trying to format the buffer.

configuration.title: ctags-enable-extra-highlighting [ yes / no ]
configuration.body: Whether or not tags found in buffers should be highlighted.

configuration.title: ctags-compl [ yes / no ]
configuration.body: Whether or not to create a completion source for tags.

commands.title: ctags-gen
commands.body: Run "ctags <ctags-flags>" as a subprocess in the background to generate/update a tags file.

commands.title: ctags-find
commands.body:
An interactive command that searches through the loaded tags in the *ctags-find buffer.
After filtering tags, ENTER can be pressed in the buffer to jump to the selected tag.

commands.title: ctags-jump-to-definition
commands.body: Jumps to the file and line containing the definition of the tag under your cursor.

commands.title: ctags-show-function-hint
commands.body: If the cursor is in the parentheses of a function call, try to lookup the function in the tags and show argument hints.

commands.title: ctags-dismiss-function-hint
commands.body: Dismiss the current function argument hint popup.

commands.title: ctags-reparse
commands.body: If ctags-enable-extra-highlighting or ctags-compl is enabled, this command reparses the tags file to refresh the list of tags.

notes: ctags must be installed on your system for this plugin to work.

name: cursor_word_hl
description: Highlight occurences of the word under the cursor.

configuration.title: cursor-word-hl-idle-threshold-ms <ms>
configuration.body: The number of milliseconds the cursor can be idle before the word under the cursor is highlighted.

notes: Uses the "associate" style component.

name: custom_frames
description: Gives a configurable framework to attach custom frames and special animations to special buffers

commands.title: set-custom-buffer-frame (frame-name[""]) (split/float['f']) (close-after[0/1]) (max-x[0.0/1.0]) (max-y[0.0/1.0]) (max-width[0.0/1.0]) (max-height[0.0/1.0])
commands.body: (use-animation[0/1]) (min-x[0.0/1.0]) (min-y[0.0/1.0]) (min-width[0.0/1.0]) (min-height[0.0/1.0]) (speed[int]) (buff-arr["*buff *buff *buff"])

commands.title: set-custom-buffer-frame (frame-name[""]) (split/float['s']) (close-after[0/1]) (max-split['h'/'v']) (max-loc[('t'/'b')/('l'/'r')]) (max-use-root['r'/'a'])
commands.body: (max-size[0.0/1.0]) (max-heirarchy[int]) (use-animation[0/1]) (min-size[0.0/1.0]) (speed[int]) (buff-arr["*buff *buff *buff"])

commands.title: special-buffer-prepare-focus-custom "*buffer_name" Overloading this function allows the user to manipulate the default way a new frame is opened for a *buffer.
special-buffer-prepare-focus "*buffer_name" This function is overloaded and must not be manipulated elsewhere.
special-buffer-prepare-jump-focus "*buffer_name" This function is overloaded and must not be manipulated elsewhere.
special-buffer-prepare-unfocus "*buffer_name" This function is overloaded and must not be manipulated elsewhere.

notes:
The way that you use this plugin is as follows. Add a configuration line in your yedrc for every frame that you want to behave a certain way.
You then can set up the parameters of how that frame should function and then what buffers you want to open in that type of frame. I will describe
the parameters now.
### frame-name: "" [""] "      " The new frame's name that will behave in a specific pattern when a linked buffer is opened or activated.
### split/float: ['s' / 'f'] Wether you want the new frame to be a split of a frame or a new "floating" frame.
### close-after: [0 / 1] "   " Wether you want to close the frame when another frame is activated.
### --------------------
### if split/float == 'f'
### "    " max-x: "    " [0.0 - 1.0] The starting x position of the "full sized" frame. 0.0 is far left and 1.0 is far right.
### "    " max-y: "    " [0.0 - 1.0] The starting y position of the "full sized" frame. 0.0 is far left and 1.0 is far right.
### "    " max-width: "" [0.0 - 1.0] The width of the "full sized" frame.
### "    " max-height: [0.0 - 1.0] The height of the "full sized" frame.
### --------------------
### if split/float == 's'
### "    " max-split: ['h' / 'v'] Wether the split will be along the 'h' horizontal or 'v' vertical axis.
### "    " --------------------
### "    " if max-split == 'h'
### "    " "    " max-loc: ['t' / 'b'] Wether you want to set the new frame in the 't' top or 'b' bottom of the new horizontal split.
### "    " --------------------
### "    " if max-split == 'v'
### "    " "    " max-loc: ['l' / 'r'] Wether you want to set the new frame in the 'l' left or 'r' right of the new vertical split.
### "    " --------------------
### "    " max-use-root: "" ['r' / 'a'] Wether you want the split to be off of the 'r' root frame or the 'a' active frame.
### "    " max-size: "    " [0.0 - 1.0] The width or height of the "full sized" split frame based on the max size of the root frame.
### "    " max-heirarchy: [int] "     " This sets the precidence for frame overlap. The lower the number the higher the precidence. A frame with heirarchy of 0 will allways overlap one with 1.
### --------------------
### use-animation: [0 / 1] Wether or not you want to make the frame use animations.
### --------------------
### if use-animation == 1
### "    " if split/float == 'f'
### "    " "    " min-x: "    " [0.0 - 1.0] The starting x position of the "minimum sized" frame. 0.0 is far left and 1.0 is far right.
### "    " "    " min-y: "    " [0.0 - 1.0] The starting y position of the "minimum sized" frame. 0.0 is far left and 1.0 is far right.
### "    " "    " min-width: "" [0.0 - 1.0] The width of the "minimum sized" frame.
### "    " "    " min-height: [0.0 - 1.0] The height of the "minimum sized" frame.
### "    " --------------------
### "    " if split/float == 's'
### "    " "    " min-size: " " [0.0 - 1.0] The width or height of the "minimum sized" split frame based on the min size of the root frame.
### "    " --------------------
### "    " speed: [int] The speed at which the animation will occur. This is in Hz.
### --------------------
### buff-arr: [""] A string of buffer names that are separated by a space. This is what links together a custom frame and the buffers.

name: diff
description: Interactively compare two buffers side-by-side with color-coded insertions, deletions, and inline character-level diffs.

configuration.title: diff-multi-line-compare-algorithm "         " [ "myers" / "myers_linear" / "patience" / ("histogram")]
configuration.body: sets the algorithm for comparing the file line by line

configuration.title: diff-line-compare-algorithm "               " [ "myers" / "myers_linear" / "patience" / ("histogram")]
configuration.body: sets the algorithm for comparing two like lines in the file

configuration.title: diff-min-lines-big-file "                   " [ 5000 ]
configuration.body: sets the min number of lines that dictates when the algorithm-big-file is used instead

configuration.title: diff-multi-line-compare-algorithm-big-file "" [ "myers" / "myers_linear" / "patience" / ("histogram")]
configuration.body: sets the algorithm for comparing the file line by line when a big file is used

configuration.title: diff-insert-color "                         " [ &active &blue swap ]
configuration.body: sets the background color for an inserted line

configuration.title: diff-insert-dashes-color "                  " [ &active &cyan swap ]
configuration.body: sets the background color for the inserted dashed line opposite an insert

configuration.title: diff-delete-color "                         " [ &active &blue swap ]
configuration.body: sets the background color for a deleted line

configuration.title: diff-delete-dashes-color "                  " [ &active &cyan swap ]
configuration.body: sets the background color for the inserted dashed line opposite a delete

configuration.title: diff-inner-compare-color "                  " [ &active &magenta swap ]
configuration.body: sets the background color two lines that are close to the same

configuration.title: diff-inner-compare-char-color "             " [ &active &attention swap ]
configuration.body: sets the background color for the characters that are inserted/deleted in the lines that are close (inner-compare)

configuration.title: diff-truncate-lines "                       " [ (yes) / no ]
configuration.body: if you want to truncate lines that are the same between both files

configuration.title: diff-truncate-lines-min-num "               " [ 12 ]
configuration.body: the minimum number of lines that need to be the same contiguously before truncation starts

configuration.title: diff-trunc-color "                          " [ &active &cyan ]
configuration.body: the color of the added line showing the number of lines that has been truncated in this section

configuration.title: diff-post-del-before-ins "                 " [ (yes) / no ]
configuration.body: reorder adjacent INS/DEL entries so all DELs come before INS within each change block

configuration.title: diff-post-slide-down "                     " [ (yes) / no ]
configuration.body: slide ambiguous edits downward within runs of identical lines

configuration.title: diff-post-indent-heuristic "               " [ (yes) / no ]
configuration.body: shift hunk boundaries to land on the least-indented line (blank lines, closing braces)

configuration.title: diff-post-blank-line-gravity "             " [ (yes) / no ]
configuration.body: prefer hunk boundaries that land on blank lines

configuration.title: diff-post-hunk-coalescence "               " [ (yes) / no ]
configuration.body: merge change blocks separated by a small number of equal lines into a single block

configuration.title: diff-post-hunk-coalescence-max-gap "       " [ 3 ]
configuration.body: maximum number of equal lines between two change blocks before they are merged

configuration.title: diff-post-semantic-cleanup "               " [ (yes) / no ]
configuration.body: merge change blocks when the equal gap between them is shorter than the smaller block

commands.title: diff(buffer1, buffer2) interactive diff for two buffers

buffers.title: *diff:buffer1 -- a buffer used by diff so that the original is not mutated
*diff:buffer2 -- a buffer used by diff so that the original is not mutated

notes:
### If you want to run this command from the terminal insert this into your .bashrc
### function yeddiff { yed -c \/"diff $1 $2\/"; }

name: drill
description: Modal editing a la kakoune, but lamer.

commands.title: drill-bind <mode> <keys> <command>
commands.body: Bind <keys> to <command> when in <mode>.

commands.title: drill-unbind <mode> <keys>
commands.body: Unbind <keys> in <mode>.

commands.title: drill-exit-insert
commands.body: Leave insert mode and return to normal mode.

commands.title: W
commands.body: Alias for write-buffer.

commands.title: Q
commands.body:
If you're in the only frame, quit.
Otherwise, close the frame.

commands.title: Wq
commands.body: write-buffer, then do q from above.

notes:
### Recommended config
bind "ctrl-o" jump-stack-pop

drill-bind normal "g d" multi jump-stack-push ctags-jump-to-definition

drill-bind normal "g e" 'cursor-buffer-end'

drill-bind normal "g g" 'cursor-buffer-begin'

name: extension_mapper
description: Map file extensions to file types.

commands.title: map-extension EXT FT
commands.body: Assign file type FT to buffers with extension EXT.

notes:
The file type must exist.

Examples:
    map-extension CC C++
    map-extension json Config

name: find_bracket
description: finds the connecting bracket to the on you are on

commands.title: brace-goto-other
commands.body: When the cursor is on a brace, this command finds the brace that connects to it and moves the cursor there.

notes: This plugin is much like the % command in vim.

name: find_file
description: Search for files in your current directory tree.

configuration.title: find-file-prg <command>
configuration.body:
The command to run where a non-backslash-escaped % is replaced with the search string.
The default value is "find . -path ./.git -prune -o -type f -name '*%*' -print".

commands.title: find-file [string]
commands.body:
If string is provided, run the find-file-prg with the string and put results in *find-file-list.
If string is not provided, find-file will be run interactively as you type the string on the command line.
Hitting ENTER on an item in *find-file-list will jump to that match.

notes:
Be careful when running find-file in high-level directories with many files.
For example you should probably avoid running find-file in your home directory because it can take a long time to search.

name: flame_graph
description: View and browse flame graph files directly in yed, useful for profiling and performance analysis.

configuration.title: flame-graph-bind-mouse [ yes / no ]
configuration.body: Whether or not to bind mouse press events for flame graph actions. Default: yes

commands.title: flame-graph [PATH]
commands.body:
Load the flame graph file at PATH for presentation.
This file must be of the same format that flamegraph.pl accepts.
A special buffer *flame-graph:PATH will be created and displayed.

commands.title: flame-graph-zoom
commands.body: Zooms in on the frame under the cursor. Bound to the ENTER key or mouse left button press (if enabled).

commands.title: flame-graph-reset-zoom
commands.body: Reset the zoom of the flame graph. Bound to the ESC key.

commands.title: flame-graph-return-zoom
commands.body: Return to the previous zoom level. Bound to the BACKSPACE key or mouse left button press on empty space (if enabled).

commands.title: flame-graph-frame-info
commands.body: Display a popup with information for the frame under the cursor. Bound to the SPACE key or mouse right button press (if enabled).

notes:
See https://www.brendangregg.com/flamegraphs.html

This implementation of flame graphs is different in one significant way: frames
are sorted left to right in descending order of sample count rather than
alphabetically. This can make it easier to quickly scan for problem paths.
Additionally, since this is drawn in the terminal, some information loss is
inevitable (it makes an effort to draw every a flame for every code path, but
there are a limited number of columns to work with). This sorting order reduces
the likelyhood that important (read high sample count) frames are lost.

Assumes truecolor support.

name: formatter
description: automatically formats C and C++ files with a self compiled version of astyle.

commands.title: run-formatter
commands.body: Formats C and C++ files.

notes:
This plugin is much like the gg=G command in vim. If you desire to specify a formatting style
Look at the man page for astyle the file that defines this is .astylerc and is located inside of $HOME

name: fstyle
description: Create and activate styles in plaintext.

commands.title: fstyle [PATH]
commands.body: If PATH is provided, load the style from the file at PATH and activate it. Otherwise, use the PATH of the current buffer.

commands.title: fstyle-export
commands.body:
If the buffer in the active frame is an fstyle buffer, export the style as C code that can be compiled into a plugin.
If the style is named, for example, "test.fstyle", the exported file will be called "test.c".

commands.title: fstyle-from STYLE [PATH]
commands.body: Convert the style loaded with the name STYLE to an fstyle file at PATH if provided, otherwise at STYLE.fstyle.

notes:
Creates the fstyle file type.
When fstyle files are edited in yed with this plugin loaded, the buffer shows each line in the attributes it is setting and updates them as you edit.
Here is an example of the syntax:

```
    active               fg c9bfb6    bg 113537
    active-border        fg 7180ac    bg 113537
    active-gutter        fg c9bfb6    bg 113537
    inactive             fg c9bfb6    bg 113537
    inactive-border      fg 7180ac    bg 113537
    inactive-gutter      fg c9bfb6    bg 113537
    cursor-line                       bg 355070
    selection                         bg 355070
    search               fg 49d45c    bg 170326    inverse
    search-cursor        fg d4495c    bg 170326    inverse
    attention            fg c00000                 bold
    associate            fg c9bfb6    bg 900040
    status-line          fg 252730    bg 7180ac    bold
    command-line         fg c9bfb6    bg 113537
    popup                fg c9bfb6    bg 900040
    popup-alt            fg c9bfb6    bg 900040    inverse
    good                 fg 009000
    bad                  fg e00000
    code-comment         fg 909090                 bold
    code-keyword         fg 8bbeb2
    code-control-flow    fg 8bbeb2
    code-typename        fg f2af29
    code-preprocessor    fg 6153cc                 bold
    code-fn-call         fg 8cBcd9
    code-number          fg e56b6f
    code-constant        fg 6153cc                 bold
    code-field           fg a99fc6
    code-variable        fg a99fc6
    code-string          fg dcccff
    code-character       fg dcccff
    code-escape          fg ef009f
```

When the style is loaded, the basename of its file will be used as the name.
For example, ~/.config/yed/styles/cubic.fstyle will be named "cubic".

name: git_variables
description: A plugin that makes variables for your current git repository properties

configuration.title: git-variables-update-interval " " [positive int miliseconds]
configuration.body: Milisecond interval to update on.

configuration.title: git-variables-branch-icon " " [string]
configuration.body: String to append to the beginning of the branch variable.

notes: Creates variable git-variables-branch which contains the current branch name.Also creates git-variables-repo-name with the repository name.

name: go_menu
description: A popup menu listing all open buffers for quickly jumping between them. Supports persistent items that always appear in the list.

configuration.title: go-menu-persistent-items [ paths... ]
configuration.body:
A space-delimited list of paths that should always be in the go-menu, whether they're open in buffers or not.
These items are always shown at the bottom of the menu.

configuration.title: go-menu-modified [YES / NO]
configuration.body: If you want to display the modified tag next to go-menu items.

commands.title: go-menu
commands.body: Open the go-menu in a new frame. The frame is acquired by calling special-buffer-prepare-focus.

notes:
When the destination buffer is a special buffer, the buffer will be opened in the frame containing the go-menu.
When the destination buffer is not special, it will be opened in the frame that was active before go-menu was called.

name: gofmt
description: Automatically format Go files with gofmt on save.

configuration.title: gofmt-auto <"yes|no"> - Automatically format buffers, off by default.

name: grep
description: Search for matches to regular expressions in files in your current directory.

configuration.title: grep-prg <command>
configuration.body:
The grep command to run where a non-backslash-escaped % is replaced with the search string.
The default value is "grep --exclude-dir={.git} -RHnIs '%' ."

commands.title: grep [regex]
commands.body:
If regex is provided, run the grep-prg with the regex and put results in *grep-list.
If regex is not provided, grep will be run interactively as you type the regex on the command line.
Hitting ENTER on an item in *grep-list will jump to that match.

notes:
Be careful when running grep in high-level directories with many files.
For example you should probably avoid running grep in your home directory because it can take a long time to search.

name: hook
description: Run yed commands automatically in response to editor events like buffer writes, frame changes, or mode switches.

commands.title: hook [options] <event> <command>
commands.body: Run <command> when <event> fires.

commands.title: hook-list-options
commands.body: Print the available options to *log.

commands.title: hook-list-events
commands.body: Print the available events to the command line.

notes: This plugin is just a proof of concept at this point, but should be developed out more eventually.

name: indent_c
description: Automatic indentation based on C-style languages.

commands.title: indent
commands.body: Move the selected line(s) to the right one indentation level.

commands.title: unindent
commands.body: Move the selected line(s) to the left one indentation level.

notes:
Uses the variable tab-width.

indent_c automatically increases and decreases the indentation level based on curly braces.

name: julie
description: Interactive editing/execution of the Julie programming language.

configuration.title: julie-interactive-file <path>
configuration.body:
Path to the file that this plugin will watch for edits.
Default value is '<your yed config path>/julie.j'.

configuration.title: julie-live-update <yes/no>
configuration.body:
If truthy, this plugin will watch for edits to julie-interactive-file and
attempt to execute the script after a short period of inactivity (500 ms).
Otherwise, the script will be executed on buffer-write.

name: jump_stack
description: Adds buffer locations to a stack so that they can be jumped back through later.

commands.title: jump-stack-push
commands.body: Push the current cursor location to the jump stack.

commands.title: jump-stack-pop
commands.body: Pop the top location from the stack and jump to it.

name: lang/c
description: Filetype for C.
notes:
The filetype is called "C".
Matches file extensions .c and .h.

name: lang/conf
description: Filetype for basic config files.
notes:
The filetype is called "Config".
Matches file extensions .conf and .config.

name: lang/cpp
description: Filetype for C++.
notes:
The filetype is called "C++".
Matches file extensions .cpp, .cxx, .hpp, .hxx, and .h.
If the extension is .h, a quick scan of the buffer see if it "looks like" C++ before setting the filetype to C++.

name: lang/csharp
description: Filetype for C#.
notes:
The filetype is called "C#".
Matches the file extensions .cs.

name: lang/glsl
description: Filetype for GLSL.
notes:
The filetype is called "GLSL".
Matches file extension .glsl.

name: lang/javascript
description: Filetype for JavaScript.
notes:
The filetype is called "JavaScript".
Matches file extension .js.

name: lang/jgraph
description: Filetype for Jgraph.
notes:
The filetype is called "Jgraph".
Matches file extension .jgr.

name: lang/julie
description: Filetype for Julie.
notes:
The filetype is called "Julie".
Matches the file extensions .j.

name: lang/latex
description: Filetype for LaTeX.
notes:
The filetype is called "LaTeX".
Matches file extensions .tex and .cls.

name: lang/linker

notes:
The filetype is called "Linker".
Matches file extension .lds.

name: lang/make

notes:

name: lang/man

configuration.title: lang-man-extensions: a space separated string of additional extensions that are considered a manpage file.

notes:
The filetype is called "Man".
Matches buffer names *.1 through *.9.

name: lang/nickel
description: Filetype for Nickel.
notes:
The filetype is called "Nickel".
Matches the file extensions .nickel.

name: lang/python
description: Filetype for Python.
notes:
The filetype is called "Python".
Matches file extension .py.

name: lang/rust
description: Filetype for Rust.
notes:
The filetype is called "Rust".
Matches file extensions .rs.

name: lang/s
description: Filetype for bpftrace files.

configuration.title: lang-bpftrace-extensions: a space separated string of all extensions that are considered a bpftrace file.

notes:
The filetype is called "S".
Matches buffer names *.S

name: lang/scheme
description: Filetype for Go.
notes:
The filetype is called "Go".
Matches the file extensions .go.

name: lang/scheme
description: Filetype for Scheme.
notes:
The filetype is called "Scheme".
Matches the file extensions .scm.

name: lang/sh
description: Filetype for Shell.
notes:
The filetype is called "Shell".
Matches file extension .sh.

name: lang/simon
description: Filetype for Simon.
notes:
The filetype is called "Simon".
Matches the file extensions .si.

name: lang/syntax/bpftrace
description: Syntax highlighting for the bpftrace filetype.

name: lang/syntax/c
description: Syntax highlighting for the C filetype.

name: lang/syntax/conf
description: Syntax highlighting for the Conf filetype.

name: lang/syntax/cpp
description: Syntax highlighting for the C++ filetype.

name: lang/syntax/csharp
description: Syntax highlighting for the C# filetype.

name: lang/syntax/csharp
description: Syntax highlighting for the Go filetype.

name: lang/syntax/diff_hl
description: Syntax highlighting for the diff files.

configuration.title: diff-hl-location the color to highlight the location line, defaults to "fg !6"
diff-hl-add "    " the color to highlight the add line, defaults to "fg !2"
diff-hl-remove " " the color to highlight the remove line, defaults to "fg !1"

name: lang/syntax/glsl
description: Syntax highlighting for the GLSL filetype.

name: lang/syntax/javascript
description: Syntax highlighting for the JavaScript filetype.

name: lang/syntax/jgraph
description: Syntax highlighting for the Jgraph filetype.

name: lang/syntax/julie
description: Syntax highlighting for the Julie filetype.

name: lang/syntax/latex
description: Syntax highlighting for the LaTeX filetype.

name: lang/syntax/linker
description: Syntax highlighting for the Linker filetype.

name: lang/syntax/make
description: Syntax highlighting for the Make filetype.

name: lang/syntax/man
description: Syntax highlighting for the Man filetype.

name: lang/syntax/nickel
description: Syntax highlighting for the Nickel filetype.

name: lang/syntax/python
description: Syntax highlighting for the Python filetype.

name: lang/syntax/rust
description: Syntax highlighting for the Rust filetype.

name: lang/syntax/scheme
description: Syntax highlighting for the Scheme filetype.

name: lang/syntax/sh
description: Syntax highlighting for the Shell filetype.

name: lang/syntax/simon
description: Syntax highlighting for the Simon filetype.

name: lang/syntax/yedrc
description: Syntax highlighting for the yedrc filetype.

name: lang/tools/latex
description: Commands to compile and view the LaTeX file you're editing.

configuration.title: latex-comp-prg <command>
configuration.body: The compile command to run where a non-backslash-escaped % is replaced with the current buffer name.

configuration.title: latex-view-prg <command>
configuration.body: The shell command used to launch a pdf viewer.

configuration.title: latex-update-view-prg <command>
configuration.body: The command used to tell the viewer to update.

commands.title: latex-compile-current-file
commands.body: Run latex-comp-prg and then latex-update-view-prg for the current file.

commands.title: latex-view-current-file
commands.body: Run latex-view-prg for the current file.

notes: Requires the LaTeX filetype to be available (generally from lang/latex).

name: lang/yedrc
description: Filetype for yedrc.
notes:
The filetype is called "yedrc".
Matches file extension .yedrc.

name: line_numbers
description: Show line numbers in the left frame gutter.
notes: Uses the "code_comment" style component to highlight numbers.

name: loc_history
description: saves cursor location between one use of YED and another.
notes: The file that this plugin uses to store the cursor locations is ~/.yed/my_loc_hitstory.txt.

name: log_hl
description: Adds color highlighting to the *log special buffer, making it easier to read editor messages and debug output.
notes: This plugin activates automatically. Open the log with buffer *log to see highlighted output.

name: lsp
description: The core Language Server Protocol client for yed. Connects to language servers to enable IDE features like go-to-definition, hover info, and diagnostics.

configuration.title: lsp-debug-log [ON/OFF]
configuration.body: Whether or not to log debug messages. Defaults to "OFF".

commands.title: lsp-define-server NAME CMD FT...
commands.body:
Define a server configuration named NAME.
The server will be started by running CMD.
Files matching any of those in FT will be managed by this server connection.
Example, using ccls for C and C++ buffers:

```
    lsp-define-server CCLS ccls C C++
```

notes:
### For users:

This plugin (or an alternative LSP controller plugin) is required for LSP-based feature plugins to function.
This plugin does not provide features such as completion, jump to definition, etc.
### For LSP feature plugin authors:

This plugin makes use of EVENT_PLUGIN_MESSAGE to communicate on behalf of LSP feature plugins to and from the language server.
When the server sends a notification, they will be broadcast with event->plugin_message.plugin_id of "lsp" and
a event->plugin_message.message_id corresponding to the LSP method received from the server.

If your feature plugin would like to make a request to the server and get the response back, you may send an EVENT_PLUGIN_MESSAGE
with a event->plugin_message.message_id of "lsp-request:METHOD" where METHOD is the server method defined by LSP.
This plugin will respond in another EVENT_PLUGIN_MESSAGE with event->plugin_message.plugin_id of "lsp" and the corresponding
method in event->plugin_message.message_id. Example flow:

```
    your plugin     -> EVENT_PLUGIN_MESSAGE{ plugin_id: "lsp_info_popup", message_id: "lsp-request:textDocument/hover" } -> this plugin
    this plugin     -> JSON request                                                                                      -> language server
    language server -> JSON response                                                                                     -> this plugin
    this plugin     -> EVENT_PLUGIN_MESSAGE{ plugin_id: "lsp", message_id: "textDocument/hover" }                        -> broadcast
```

The response from the server will be in found as a JSON string in event->plugin_message.string_data.
In most cases, you should cancel the event with the response corresponding to the request you made.
Otherwise, another plugin may recieve that event and duplicate behavior (this may be desired).

name: lsp_diagnostics
description: Displays inline errors, warnings, and linting messages from language servers directly in your code as you type.

configuration.title: lsp-diagnostics-show-message [ never, cursor, or always ]
configuration.body:
When to show diagnostic messages next the the offending line(s).

"never": Never show the diagnostics in the frame.

"cursor": Show messages when the cursor is within the offending region.

"always": Always show messages.

Defaults to "always".

buffers.title: *lsp-diagnostics
buffers.body: A jumpable list of locations with a corresponding diagnostic.

notes: Requires an LSP controller plugin.

name: lsp_info_popup
description: LSP-provided information about a source location in a popup.

configuration.title: lsp-info-popup-idle-threshold-ms MILLISECONDS
configuration.body:
Automatically popup information after the cursor has been idle for MILLISECONDS.

< 0: Never automatically show the popup.

0: Always show the popup.

> 0: Show the popup after the cursor has been idle for this amount of time.

Defaults to 1500 (1.5 seconds).

commands.title: lsp-info
commands.body: Summon the popup for the current location (cursor).

notes: Requires an LSP controller plugin.

name: lsp-symbol-buffer
description: Jump to declarations, definitions, and references via LSP. Includes an interactive symbol search with tab completion and a symbol menu showing all results in one view.

configuration.title: lsp-symbol-menu-function-color [ &magenta ]
configuration.body: The color for functions in the menu.

configuration.title: lsp-symbol-menu-symbol-color [ &red swap ]
configuration.body: The color for symbols in the menu.

configuration.title: lsp-symbol-menu-declaration-color [ &gray swap ]
configuration.body: The color for declarations in the menu.

configuration.title: lsp-symbol-menu-definition-color [ &gray swap ]
configuration.body: The color for definitions in the menu.

configuration.title: lsp-symbol-menu-reference-color [ &gray swap ]
configuration.body: The color for references in the menu.

commands.title: lsp-search-symbol
commands.body:
Place the cursor over a symbol and run this command.
Queries the LSP server for the declaration, definition, and all references of the symbol under the cursor.
Results are displayed in the *symbol-menu buffer.
Hitting ENTER on a result line jumps to that location.

commands.title: lsp-search-txt <name>
commands.body:
Search for a symbol by name rather than by cursor position.
Sends a workspace/symbol query to the LSP server, populates the definition directly from the result, then queries for declaration and references.
Results are displayed in the *symbol-menu buffer.
Tab completion is available and draws from LSP symbol names and words in the current buffer.

commands.title: lsp-find-symbol
commands.body:
Opens an interactive search interface in the *lsp-find-symbol buffer.
Type characters to filter the list of known symbols and buffer words.
Only symbols whose names begin with the typed prefix are shown, sorted alphabetically.
Hitting ENTER sends the typed text to lsp-search-txt.
Hitting ESC or Ctrl-C cancels and closes the buffer.

commands.title: lsp-goto-declaration
commands.body: Navigate directly to the declaration of the symbol under the cursor.

commands.title: lsp-goto-definition
commands.body: Navigate directly to the definition of the symbol under the cursor.

commands.title: lsp-find-references
commands.body: Find all references of the symbol under the cursor and populate *symbol-menu.

commands.title: lsp-goto-next-reference
commands.body: After running lsp-find-references, jump to the next reference in the cycle.

commands.title: lsp-goto-prev-reference
commands.body: After running lsp-find-references, jump to the previous reference in the cycle.

buffers.title: *symbol-menu
buffers.body:
Displays the declaration, definition, and references for the most recently searched symbol.
Layout: rows 6-7 = Declaration, rows 9-10 = Definition, rows 12+ = References.
Hitting ENTER on a result line jumps to that location.

buffers.title: *lsp-find-symbol
buffers.body:
Interactive search buffer used by lsp-find-symbol.
Shows up to 50 matching symbol names as you type, sorted alphabetically.

notes:
This plugin requires the yed-lsp plugin to be loaded and an LSP server to be configured for the current file type.
Use lsp-define-server to configure an LSP server.

For C and C++ files, clangd with --background-index is recommended.
A compile_commands.json file in the project root is required for clangd to index the project.
Use bear -- make (or cmake with -DCMAKE_EXPORT_COMPILE_COMMANDS=1) to generate it.

lsp-search-txt and lsp-find-symbol query workspace/symbol to locate symbols by name.
The definition is populated directly from the workspace/symbol result.
Declaration and references are queried separately and may be empty if the LSP server has not fully indexed the project.

name: macro
description: Record a sequence of keystrokes and play them back to repeat complex editing operations across your buffer.

configuration.title: macro-stop-key [key-string]
configuration.body:
The key designated to mean "stop recording the macro".
Default value is ctrl-e.

configuration.title: macro-instant-playback [ yes / no ]
configuration.body:
When this variable is set to "yes", keystrokes will be replayed instantly -- i.e. you won't see them happen.
Default value is "no".
Otherwise keystrokes will be replayed once per editor pump.

commands.title: macro-record
commands.body: Start recording a macro.

commands.title: macro-play [ N ]
commands.body:
If N is provided, play the macro N times.
Otherwise play the macro once for each line selected (once if no selection).

name: make-tab
description: Automatically inserts real tab characters instead of spaces when editing Makefiles, which require tabs for indentation.
notes: This plugin activates automatically for files with the Make filetype. No configuration needed.

name: man
description: Open and view man pages in the special buffer *man-page.

commands.title: man <arguments>
commands.body: Run "man <arguments>" as a subprocess and collect the output in the special buffer *man-page.

commands.title: man-word
commands.body: Equivalent to running "man word-under-cursor".

name: mouse
description: Adds basic mouse support to yed — click to place the cursor, scroll to navigate, and interact with UI elements.

configuration.title: mouse-cursor-scroll
configuration.body: Sets whether or not the scrolling affects the cursor or not.

configuration.title: mouse-scroll-num-lines
configuration.body: Sets the number of lines each movent of the scroll wheel moves the cursor.

notes: This plugin adds left click support to include selecting a location in a buffer as well as activating a frame. It also allows the user to scrol the buffer. If you double click it will hit enter on the cursor location.

name: mouse_frame_control
description: allows the user to move and resize frames with the middle mouse button
notes: Clicking in the lower right corner with the middle button allows the user to drag to resize the frame. Clicking anywhere elese on the frame allows the user to move it.

name: mouse-menu
description: Adds a configurable right-click context menu to yed with customizable actions for words, selections, and empty space.

configuration.title: mouse-menu-on-word
configuration.body: This configuration sets the menu when right clicking on a word and takes a string of space separated tuples where the first thing is the name of the line in the menu and the second thing is the command it will run such as "Paste paste-yank-buffer"

configuration.title: mouse-menu-on-selection
configuration.body: This configuration sets the menu when right clicking and a selection is active and takes a string of space separated tuples where the first thing is the name of the line in the menu and the second thing is the command it will run such as "Copy yank-selection"

configuration.title: mouse-menu-on-nothing
configuration.body: This configuration sets the menu when right clicking on the background and takes a string of space separated tuples where the first thing is the name of the line in the menu and the second thing is the command it will run such as "'Frame New' frame-new"

name: nix
description: Filetype for Nix.
notes:
The filetype is called "Nix".
Matches file extension .nix.

name: paren_hl
description: Visually highlights the matching parenthesis when your cursor is on one, making it easy to navigate nested expressions.

configuration.title: paren-hl-max-line-length
configuration.body: When scanning for matching parens, if a line exceeds paren-hl-max-line-length, stop scanning.

notes: Uses the "associate" style component.

name: pastebin
description: Simple plugin for uploading your selection to a pastebin service.

configuration.title: pastebin-fav - Specify which service to use, the current options are listed next.
configuration.body:
\h'-04'\(bu\h'+03'\c
\(bu 4. 
ixio - Uses ix.io
\h'-04'\(bu\h'+03'\c
\(bu 4. 
paste-rs - Uses https://paste.rs
\h'-04'\(bu\h'+03'\c
\(bu 4. 
dpaste - Uses https://dpaste.org/
\h'-04'\(bu\h'+03'\c
\(bu 4. 
mozilla - Uses https://paste.mozilla.org/

pastebin-url - Variable that shows the last url returned by pastebin.

commands.title: pastebin
commands.body: Uploads selection to pastebin.

notes: Defaults to paste.rs

name: profile
description: Generate a profile report of the time spent in yed commands.

commands.title: profile-start
commands.body: Begin profiling yed commands.

commands.title: profile-end
commands.body: Stop profiling yed commands.

commands.title: profile-report
commands.body: Focus the *profile buffer.

notes:
The time reported for each command does only does not include the time spent in commands called within the first command unless they are the same command.
This plugin is useful for finding out which parts of your yed configuration are taking up time in, for example, startup.

name: save_layout
description: Saves the layout of the current frame / buffer configuration to a file.

commands.title: save-current-yed-layout saves the current frame / buffer configuration to the file my_yed_layout.yedrc in ~/.config/yed.
save-current-yed-layout-local saves the current frame / buffer configuration to the file my_yed_layout.yedrc in the local directory.

name: scroll_buffer
description: Move the buffer up/down lines while keeping the cursor in place.

commands.title: scroll-buffer <int>
commands.body: Scroll the buffer some number of lines. If the argument is negative, the buffer is scrolled up. Positive down.

notes: The cursor must always be in frame, so if the desired scroll would take the cursor out of view in the frame, the cursor is moved as well.

name: shell_run
description: Run a shell command in the background and collect its output in the special buffer *shell-output.

commands.title: shell-run <command>
commands.body: Run <command> in the background and focus *shell-output.

notes:
Interactive commands should not be run with this command because the command will not receive any input.
Additionally, terminal escape codes in the command's output will not be interpreted as such.

name: style_pack
description: A convenient bundle of most available color themes in a single plugin. Install this to get a wide selection of styles to try.
notes: After loading, use the style command or the style_picker plugin to browse and activate themes. All bundled styles support truecolor.

name: style_picker
description: An interactive buffer *style-picker-list that allows you to test styles that you have loaded.

commands.title: style-picker
commands.body:
Focuses *style-picker-list.
Pressing ENTER on a style will activate the style.
Leaving the frame will revert back to the style activated before style-picker was run.

name: style_use_term_bg
description: Load/change styles to use the background color specified in your terminal. Useful for semi-transparent terminal backgrounds.

commands.title: style-use-term-bg
commands.body: Change the current style to use the terminal background color.

commands.title: style-term-bg <style>
commands.body: Activate <style>, but change it to use the terminal background color.

name: styles/acme
description: A light truecolor theme inspired by the Plan 9 Acme editor. Warm cream background with clean, readable colors.

name: styles/alchemist
description: A dark truecolor theme with deep teal tones and a mysterious, muted palette.

name: styles/blue
description: A classic 16-color theme with a blue background. Works in any terminal.

name: styles/bold
description: Two 256-color themes (bold-dark and bold-light) with strong contrast and vivid syntax colors.

name: styles/book
description: A 16-color theme with a muted gray background, easy on the eyes for long reading sessions.

name: styles/bullet
description: A dark truecolor theme with a near-black background and sharp, high-contrast syntax colors.

name: styles/cadet
description: A dark truecolor theme with deep navy tones and cool blue accents.

name: styles/casey
description: A dark truecolor theme with warm, earthy tones inspired by Handmade Hero.

name: styles/crunchy_leaves
description: A dark truecolor theme with warm autumn colors — oranges, browns, and deep reds.

name: styles/dalton
description: A dark truecolor theme with a deep purple-black background and vibrant accents.

name: styles/disco
description: A dark truecolor theme with deep blue tones and colorful syntax highlighting.

name: styles/doug
description: A dark truecolor theme with a slate-gray background and balanced, comfortable colors.

name: styles/dracula
description: A dark truecolor theme based on the popular Dracula color scheme. Purple, pink, and cyan on a dark background.

name: styles/drift
description: A dark truecolor theme with a deep navy background and subtle, cool-toned syntax colors.

name: styles/elise
description: A dark truecolor theme with a teal-tinted background and soft, muted colors.

name: styles/elly
description: A dark truecolor theme with a deep blue-gray background and gentle contrast.

name: styles/embark
description: A dark truecolor theme with rich purple undertones and vibrant accents.

name: styles/first
description: The original yed themes — first-dark and first-light. The dark variant uses deep navy with teal and cream.

name: styles/forest
description: A dark truecolor theme with deep green tones, like coding in a quiet forest.

name: styles/gruvbox

name: styles/moss

name: styles/mrjantz
description: A dark truecolor theme with a pure black background and vivid, high-contrast syntax colors.

name: styles/nord
description: A dark truecolor theme based on the Nord color palette. Cool arctic blues and clean, frosty tones.

name: styles/olive
description: A dark truecolor theme with deep forest-green tones and warm olive accents.

name: styles/papercolor
description: A light truecolor theme based on the PaperColor color scheme. Clean and bright with good readability.

name: styles/pastel
description: A dark truecolor theme with soft, muted pastel syntax colors on a dark gray background.

name: styles/river
description: A dark truecolor theme with steel-blue tones, like light reflecting off water.

name: styles/sammy
description: A dark truecolor theme with a deep blue-gray background and balanced, pleasant colors.

name: styles/skyfall
description: A dark truecolor theme with a near-black background and cool, understated syntax colors.

name: styles/solarized
description: A dark truecolor theme based on the iconic Solarized color scheme by Ethan Schoonover.

name: styles/tempus_future
description: A dark truecolor theme with a futuristic, high-tech feel and sharp color contrast.

name: styles/vt
description: A dark truecolor theme styled after classic VT terminal aesthetics — green and amber on black.

name: styles/vt_light

name: styles/zulu

name: sysclip
description: Copy yed yank buffer contents to the system clipboard.

configuration.title: Variables
configuration.body:
\h'-04'\(bu\h'+03'\c
\(bu 4. 
sys-clip - Specify your system clipboard.

commands.title: y2s - Stands for yank2system, yanks to your specified system clipboard defaults to xsel.

notes: Clipboards that fork will hang, like wl-clipboard, so do not use them, or at least turn off the forking if possible.

name: terminal
description: A full terminal emulator running inside a yed buffer. Open a shell, run commands, and switch back to editing without leaving yed.

configuration.title: terminal-shell
configuration.body: The child process to start. Defaults to the value of $SHELL.

configuration.title: terminal-termvar
configuration.body:
This determines what the value of $TERM is to child process.
The default value is xterm-256color.
It is not advised to change this unless you know what you're doing.

configuration.title: terminal-scrollback
configuration.body:
The number of lines to hold as available scrollback in the terminal buffer.
The default value is 10000.

configuration.title: terminal-max-block-size
configuration.body:
The maximum number of bytes that can be read from the child process before forcing an update
to the buffer.
The default value is 16384.

configuration.title: terminal-read-chunk-size
configuration.body:
The size of the buffer used to read the child process's output.
The default value is 1024.

configuration.title: terminal-auto-term-mode
configuration.body:
If this variable is truthy, term mode will be automatically turned back on when activating a frame with a terminal buffer in it.
Defaults to ON.

configuration.title: terminal-show-welcome
configuration.body:
If this variable is truthy, the YED TERMINAL message will be printed when a new terminal starts.
Defaults to yes.

configuration.title: terminal-debug-log
configuration.body:
If this variable is truthy, debug log messages will be printed about terminal state and control sequences.
Defaults to OFF.

configuration.title: terminal-color0
configuration.body: Attribute string to use for color0. Defaults to &black.

configuration.title: terminal-color1
configuration.body: Attribute string to use for color1. Defaults to &red.

configuration.title: terminal-color2
configuration.body: Attribute string to use for color2. Defaults to &green.

configuration.title: terminal-color3
configuration.body: Attribute string to use for color3. Defaults to &yellow.

configuration.title: terminal-color4
configuration.body: Attribute string to use for color4. Defaults to &blue.

configuration.title: terminal-color5
configuration.body: Attribute string to use for color5. Defaults to &magenta.

configuration.title: terminal-color6
configuration.body: Attribute string to use for color6. Defaults to &cyan.

configuration.title: terminal-color7
configuration.body: Attribute string to use for color7. Defaults to &gray.

configuration.title: terminal-color8
configuration.body: Attribute string to use for color8. Defaults to &gray.

configuration.title: terminal-color9
configuration.body: Attribute string to use for color9. Defaults to &red.

configuration.title: terminal-color10
configuration.body: Attribute string to use for color10. Defaults to &green.

configuration.title: terminal-color11
configuration.body: Attribute string to use for color11. Defaults to &yellow.

configuration.title: terminal-color12
configuration.body: Attribute string to use for color12. Defaults to &blue.

configuration.title: terminal-color13
configuration.body: Attribute string to use for color13. Defaults to &magenta.

configuration.title: terminal-color14
configuration.body: Attribute string to use for color14. Defaults to &cyan.

configuration.title: terminal-color15
configuration.body: Attribute string to use for color15. Defaults to &white.

configuration.title: terminal-color-default
configuration.body: Attribute string to use for deafult text. Defaults to &active.

configuration.title: terminal-color-default-inactive
configuration.body: Attribute string to use for deafult text when the terminal is not in the active frame. Defaults to &inactive.

commands.title: term-new
commands.body: Open a new terminal buffer. New terminal buffers are named *term#, where # is a positive integer starting at 0.

commands.title: term-open [#]
commands.body:
Opens a terminal buffer and displays in a frame acquired by calling special-buffer-prepare-focus.
If # is provided, use *term# (creating it if it doesn't exist).
Otherwise, a new terminal is created.

commands.title: term-open-no-frame [#]
commands.body: This command behaves the same as term-open, but does not call special-buffer-prepare-focus and instead uses the currently active frame.

commands.title: toggle-term-mode
commands.body: Turn term mode ON/OFF for the current terminal buffer. See NOTES for information about term mode.

commands.title: term-mode-off BUFFER
commands.body: Turn term mode OFF for the terminal buffer BUFFER.

commands.title: term-mode-on BUFFER
commands.body: Turn term mode ON for the terminal buffer BUFFER.

commands.title: term-feed-keys BUFFER KEYS...
commands.body:
Send each key in KEYS to the terminal buffer BUFFER.

Example: term-feed-keys *term0 l s enter

commands.title: term-feed-text BUFFER TEXT
commands.body:
Send each character in TEXT to the terminal buffer BUFFER.

Example: term-feed-text *term0 "ls\\n"

commands.title: term-bind KEY CMD ARGS...
commands.body: Bind KEY to execute CMD ARGS... when in a terminal and in term mode.

commands.title: term-unbind KEY
commands.body: Remove terminal key binding for KEY.

notes:
Notable Features:
tab(@);
| c | c |
| l | l |.
_
SUPPORTED@UNSUPPORTED
_
Truecolor@SGR Mouse Reporting
Bracketed Paste Mode@Italic text
OSC 52 pass-through@Sixel Graphics
_

A terminal buffer in "term mode" behaves less like a yed buffer and more like a terminal.
Keys presses go through the terminal's input handling and mostly bypass other yed/plugin functionality.
If term mode is turned off via toggle-term-mode, the buffer behaves like a normal yed buffer that can
be scrolled, yanked from, searched, and all the other nice things that you can do with yed buffers.
Note, however, that the terminal buffers are read-only (only the terminal plugin modifies the buffers),
because manipulating the contents otherwise would desynchronize the state of the terminal with the
programs running in it.

By default, ctrl-t is bound in the terminal to toggle-term-mode.

Key bindings are inserted into a new keymap called "terminal".

name: tree_view
description: A buffer that shows a tree view of all files.

configuration.title: tree-view-update-period
configuration.body: Number of seconds between automatic filesystem refresh. Default is 5.

configuration.title: tree-view-hidden-items
configuration.body:
Space-separated list of substrings. Any file or directory whose name contains
one of these substrings will be hidden from the tree view.

configuration.title: tree-view-image-extensions
configuration.body:
Space-separated list of additional file extensions to treat as image files,
supplementing the built-in list. Example: ".raw .nef"

configuration.title: tree-view-archive-extensions
configuration.body:
Space-separated list of additional file extensions to treat as archive files,
supplementing the built-in list. Example: ".squashfs .zst"

configuration.title: tree-view-child-char-l
configuration.body: Character used for the corner connector in the tree, default is "└" (└).

configuration.title: tree-view-child-char-i
configuration.body: Character used for the vertical line in the tree, default is "│" (│).

configuration.title: tree-view-child-char-t
configuration.body: Character used for the branch connector in the tree, default is "├" (├).

configuration.title: tree-view-directory-color
configuration.body: Attribute string for coloring directories. Default is "&blue".

configuration.title: tree-view-exec-color
configuration.body: Attribute string for coloring executable files. Default is "&green".

configuration.title: tree-view-symbolic-link-color
configuration.body: Attribute string for coloring symbolic links. Default is "&cyan".

configuration.title: tree-view-device-color
configuration.body: Attribute string for coloring device files. Default is "&black swap &yellow.fg".

configuration.title: tree-view-graphic-image-color
configuration.body: Attribute string for coloring image files. Default is "&magenta".

configuration.title: tree-view-archive-color
configuration.body: Attribute string for coloring archive files. Default is "&red".

configuration.title: tree-view-broken-link-color
configuration.body: Attribute string for coloring broken symbolic links. Default is "&black swap &red.fg".

commands.title: tree-view
commands.body:
Opens the
**tree-view-list*
buffer. If the tree has not been built yet it will be initialized first.
Press
**Enter**
on a directory to expand or collapse it. Press
**Enter**
on a file to open it in the editor.

buffers.title: *tree-view-list
buffers.body: The interactive file tree. Read-only; modified internally by the plugin.

buffers.title: *tree-view-preview*
buffers.body:
Ephemeral preview buffer. Populated on demand when hovering over a file and
cleared when the cursor leaves the file. Should not be written to directly.

notes: Extension matching is case-insensitive and anchored to the end of the filename.

extraSections.PREVIEW:
When the cursor is on a file in
**tree-view-list,*
a preview of that file is shown in a split pane to the right. Only as many
lines as fit in the preview frame are read from the file; large files are
never fully loaded. The preview buffer is read-only and is not tracked as a
normal file.

Moving the cursor to a directory or any non-regular entry closes the preview
pane automatically.
extraSections.FILE TYPE DETECTION:
Files are classified in the following priority order:
1.. 
Directory
2.. 
Symbolic link (valid or broken)
3.. 
Device file (block or character)
4.. 
Executable (owner execute bit set)
5.. 
Archive (by extension)
6.. 
Image (by extension)
7.. 
Regular file

name: universal_clipboard
description: A plugin that can copy a selection to the systems clipboard using OSC 52.

configuration.title: universal-copy-on-yank [yes/no]
configuration.body: When truthy the selection will copy to the system keyboard on any yank-selection command.

configuration.title: universal-clipboard-trim-nl [yes/no]
configuration.body: When truthy a trailing new line will be removed from the text that is copied to the systems clipboard.

commands.title: copy-to-universal-clipboard
commands.body: Copies the selection the system clipboard.

notes:
In order for this plugin to work your terminal emulator must support the osc 52 escape sequence. To test this paste the following line into a shell, if hello world is now in your clipboard, then your terminal is supported.

printf "\\033]52;c;$(printf "%s" "hello world" | base64)\\a"

Multiplexers such as screen and tmux can hinder operation. Tmux does work if you put the following two commands into your ~/.tmux.conf .

set -g set-clipboard on

set-option -ag terminal-overrides ",*:Ms=\\\\E]52;c;%p2%s\\\\7"

name: vimish
description: A modal editor experience that is like vim, but different.

commands.title: vimish-bind <mode> <keys> <command>
commands.body: Bind <keys> to <command> when in <mode>.

commands.title: vimish-unbind <mode> <keys>
commands.body: Unbind <keys> in <mode>.

commands.title: vimish-exit-insert
commands.body: Leave insert mode and return to normal mode.

commands.title: W
commands.body: Alias for write-buffer.

commands.title: Q
commands.body:
If you're in the only frame, quit.
Otherwise, close the frame.

commands.title: Wq
commands.body: write-buffer, then do q from above.

name: whence_you_came
description: Focus the previously focused buffer in a frame.

commands.title: whence-you-came
commands.body: Focus the buffer that was previously focused in the active frame.

name: word_wrap
description: Wrap all selected lines to a given number of characters.

commands.title: word-wrap <int>
commands.body:
Wrap all selected lines to <int> characters.
Most useful for writing prose: preserves paragraphs.

name: xul
description: A modal editor experience inspired by vim and kakoune. Walking selection, vim-like motions, useful operators.

commands.title: xul-bind <mode> <keys> <command>
commands.body: Bind <keys> to <command> when in <mode>.

commands.title: xul-unbind <mode> <keys>
commands.body: Unbind <keys> in <mode>.

commands.title: xul-exit-insert
commands.body: Leave insert mode and return to normal mode.

name: yedrc
description: Load text files containing yed commands and execute them.

commands.title: yedrc-load <path>
commands.body: Load the file <path> and execute each line as a yed command.

notes:
This plugin is useful for easy configuration and editor settings.
Typical usage involves loading a file like ~/.config/yed/yedrc at startup.

yedrc-load can be called again from yedrc files since it is just another command.
This allows you to have your configuration split into multiple files and have project-local configurations such as a .yedrc file in your project directory.

The syntax/quoting rules for each command are the same as they are on yed's command line except that you can
split commands into multiple lines with trailing backslashes.

yedrc-load will not recursively execute files.

FT and syntax are by default provided by the lang/yedrc and lang/syntax/yedrc plugins.

name: yule_2021
description: A festive holiday plugin that makes it snow in your editor. Because even terminal editors deserve some holiday cheer.

name: yule_2022
description: Another festive holiday plugin with a hopping surprise. A fun seasonal treat for your terminal.

name: zig
description: Filetype for Zig.
notes:
The filetype is called "Zig".
Matches file extension .zig.
